posts.title: Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy
posts.summary: European government space spending jumped 12% to about $15.4 billion in 2025, according to a July 13 European Space Agency report, bucking a 3% global decline on the back of rising national defense budgets. The post Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy appeared first on SpaceNews.

posts.title: NASA Study Points to Smoother Air Taxi Rides
posts.summary: No one wants to get into an uncomfortable aircraft. NASA research could help the emerging industry of air taxis —small, vertical-takeoff-and-landing aircraft meant for short trips — understand the relationship between comfort and willingness to fly. That’s where NASA comes in, with data that can help identify how to plan air taxi rides that can […]

posts.summary: The Space Development Agency awarded the companies a combined $1.75 billion for next batch of missile-tracking satellites The post L3Harris, Sierra Space to build 36 satellites to expand U.S. missile-tracking network appeared first on SpaceNews.

posts.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch a flight-proven Falcon booster for 600th time
posts.summary: The Starlink 10-45 mission will add another 29 broadband internet satellites to SpaceX’s low Earth orbit constellation. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for 4:27 a.m. EDT (0827 UTC).

posts.title: Poland to host new ESA center as government boosts investments in space
posts.summary: WARSAW, Poland — Poland will host the first European Space Agency (ESA) center located in an eastern flank member state, one that will be focused on civil security and resilience. […] The post Poland to host new ESA center as government boosts investments in space appeared first on SpaceNews.

posts.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch  24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg
posts.summary: The Starlink 15-14 mission will add another 24 broadband internet satellites to the low Earth orbit constellation. Liftoff from pad 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base is scheduled for 6:17 p.m. PDT (9:17 p.m. EDT / 0117 UTC).

posts.title: What happens if Crew Dragon stops flying in the 2030s?
posts.summary: "It's very clear that in the United States there is a big need for an additional crew vehicle."

posts.title: Netherlands Adopts Mandate for Its New Space Agency
posts.summary: The Netherlands has formalised the mandate of its rebranded space agency, adding the Ministry of Defence to its steering committee for the first time. The formation of the new agency was announced on 3 March at the Amsterdam Space Symposium, with the Netherlands Space Office becoming the Netherlands Space Agency (NLSA). At the time, secretary […] The post Netherlands Adopts Mandate for Its New Space Agency appeared first on European Spaceflight.

posts.title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
posts.summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon poses in a spacesuit for a portrait at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas on Jan. 8, 2026. Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition […]

posts.title: Accel and Prosus backed QOSMIC raises $3.33 Million to Build Optical Ground Stations for the Orbital Data Economy
posts.summary: BENGALURU, India / July 15, 2026 — QOSMIC has raised $3.33 million in seed funding co-led by Accel and Prosus, with participation from South Park Commons, ARTPARK, and angel investor […] The post Accel and Prosus backed QOSMIC raises $3.33 Million to Build Optical Ground Stations for the Orbital Data Economy appeared first on SpaceNews.

posts.title: ESA releases 2026 Space Economy Report
posts.summary: The European Space Agency’s (ESA) newly released Space Economy Report gives a comprehensive overview of the latest developments shaping Europe's space sector.

posts.title: El telescopio James Webb detecta vapor de agua en un exoplaneta rocoso
posts.summary: Nuevas observaciones del telescopio espacial James Webb sugieren la presencia de vapor de agua en la atmósfera de un pequeño planeta rocoso a 26 años luz, un paso importante en la búsqueda de mundos habitables.

posts.title: SpaceX lanza con éxito otra tanda de satélites Starlink
posts.summary: Un cohete Falcon 9 puso en órbita 23 satélites Starlink desde Cabo Cañaveral. La primera etapa aterrizó sin problemas en la plataforma marítima, sumando su vigésima reutilización.
